one_line: Sibling Buddhist missionaries from the Mauryan court, bringing the dhamma and the Bodhi tree cutting to ancient Sri Lanka.

appearance.age_look: Mahinda appears in his early 30s with a lean monk’s build; Sanghamitta in her late 20s with a composed, upright posture and graceful movement.
appearance.face: Mahinda has an oval face, warm medium-brown complexion, straight nose, sharp jawline, and calm, penetrating eyes with a slight upward tilt; Sanghamitta has a gently heart-shaped face, slightly lighter brown skin, soft features, rounded nose, and serene eyes; both usually hold a gentle, compassionate half-smile.
appearance.hair: Mahinda’s head is clean-shaven with faint stubble, no headwear; Sanghamitta’s hair is entirely covered by a plain white cotton headwrap snugly tied at the back of the head, with no stray strands visible.
appearance.costume: Mahinda wears a simple Theravāda monk’s robe in the Mauryan style: a saffron-ochre cotton civara draped over the left shoulder and under the right arm, layered over an inner pale brown lower garment, leaving his right shoulder and arm bare; the robe is slightly worn with soft folds, tied at the waist with a narrow fabric belt; he is barefoot, with plain wooden sandals sometimes at his feet; Sanghamitta wears a modest nun’s robe: a full-coverage pale yellow upper robe covering both shoulders, over a deep saffron lower robe that reaches her ankles, with a simple white cotton under-robe visible at the neckline, and a thin brown fabric belt; her white headwrap matches the under-robe; she is barefoot, with plain wooden sandals occasionally depicted beside her; no jewelry or ornament is worn by either, emphasizing renunciation and Mauryan-era Buddhist austerity.
appearance.signature: Mahinda carries a slender palm-leaf manuscript bundle wrapped in a faded reddish-brown cloth in his left hand, while Sanghamitta cradles a small earthen pot with a sprouting Bodhi sapling, its two or three heart-shaped green leaves clearly visible.

ref_desc: Mahinda is a lean, medium-brown, shaven-headed monk in saffron-ochre Mauryan robes with a palm-leaf manuscript bundle, and Sanghamitta is a serene, medium-brown nun in pale yellow and saffron robes with a white headwrap carrying an earthen pot holding a green Bodhi sapling.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of two fixed characters standing side by side: Mahinda, a lean medium-brown man in his early 30s with an oval face, calm eyes and a gentle half-smile, completely shaven head, wearing historically accurate Mauryan-period Theravāda monk robes—a saffron-ochre cotton civara draped over his left shoulder and under his right arm, exposing his right shoulder and arm, layered over a pale brown lower garment and tied at the waist with a narrow fabric belt, barefoot on the ground with simple wooden sandals near his feet, holding a slender palm-leaf manuscript bundle wrapped in a faded reddish-brown cloth in his left hand; and Sanghamitta, a woman in her late 20s with a heart-shaped face, serene eyes, gentle expression and medium-brown complexion, her hair fully covered by a plain white cotton headwrap snugly tied at the back, wearing a modest nun’s outfit of a pale yellow upper robe covering both shoulders over a deep saffron lower robe reaching her ankles, with a white cotton under-robe visible at the neckline and a thin brown fabric belt, barefoot with simple wooden sandals nearby, cradling in both hands a small earthen pot from which a young Bodhi sapling grows, showing a short trunk and two or three heart-shaped green leaves; keep proportions and eyes in dynamic shonen anime style but render the robes, sandals, manuscript, and Bodhi pot in realistic Mauryan-era textures and folds, using a palette dominated by saffron, ochre, pale yellow, white, earthy browns, and deep green for the leaves, clearly showing their costume details from head to toe for consistent redrawing.
